reviews.author: Lily F.
reviews.text: Better than the average Indian restaurant in London. Not as good as my mum makes.

reviews.author: Nadia S.
reviews.text: I have been 8 times. Every time has been excellent.

reviews.author: Mei C.
reviews.text: It is what it says it is. A Bombay café. In Shoreditch. With a queue.

reviews.author: Owen C.
reviews.text: Booked three months ahead. Worth every minute.

reviews.author: Riya M.
reviews.text: The interior is a love letter to mid-century Bombay. Beautiful even if you are just there for the daal.

reviews.author: Lily F.
reviews.text: Get there before 9am if you want the bacon naan at breakfast.

reviews.author: Marcus G.
reviews.text: Lamb raan is a whole-meal experience. Order it.

reviews.author: Tara P.
reviews.text: Black daal again. Worth every spoonful. Worth the queue.

reviews.author: James L.
reviews.text: Bring an empty stomach and a hungry friend. Share everything.

reviews.author: Zara Q.
reviews.text: The house lassi is dangerous. Dangerously good.

reviews.author: Aisha L.
reviews.text: The house lassi is dangerous. Dangerously good.

reviews.author: Mei C.
reviews.text: Reservations disappeared from my calendar. Showed up, they had nothing.

reviews.author: Lily F.
reviews.text: I have eaten Indian food all over London and Bombay. Dishoom is its own thing. The best thing.

reviews.author: Tara P.
reviews.text: Finally an Indian restaurant that does not dumb down. The black daal is worth the queue alone.

reviews.author: Eve M.
reviews.text: The chai is the best in London. Possibly the world.

reviews.author: Hiro T.
reviews.text: Went once, queued, ate, left. Solid Indian food.

reviews.author: Hassan Y.
reviews.text: I queued 40 minutes for a table at 11am on a Tuesday. Make it make sense.

reviews.text: Noisy. Genuinely the loudest restaurant I have eaten in this year.
